name: Mercy USA for Aid and Development
mission: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development is dedicated to alleviating human suffering and supporting individuals and their communities in their efforts to become more self-sufficient. Our philosophy is "Helping People Help Themselves".  Incorporated in the State of Michigan in 1988, Mercy-USA's projects focus on improving health, nutrition and access to safe water, as well as promoting economic and educational growth and providing disaster relief around the world.
theoryOfChange: Stated focus on alleviating human suffering and supporting individuals and communities in their efforts to become more self-sufficient, with projects focusing on improving health, nutrition, and access to safe water, and promoting economic and educational growth worldwide.
scoreSummary: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development, Inc. shows exceptional alignment and strong impact, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Registered Private Voluntary Organization (PVO) (United States Agency for International Development (USAID))
Special Consultative Status (United Nations Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC))

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://mercyusa.org/zakat (Source: https://mercyusa.org/zakat)

keyConcerns.detail: Noncash contributions inflate reported program ratios. Cash-adjusted metrics better reflect actual spending.

keyConcerns.headline: 96% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

score_details.score_summary: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development, Inc. shows exceptional alignment and strong impact, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://mercyusa.org/zakat (Source: https://mercyusa.org/zakat)

all_citations.claim: Mercy-USA has a Charity Navigator score of 92.33333333333333/100 and a program expense ratio of 96.3%.
The organization is zakat-eligible and follows specific policies for asnaf categories.
The organization reported total revenue of $34,922,507 and fundraising efficiency of $0.03 per $1 raised.
Mercy-USA provides nutrition support and health services for children and mothers in Somalia.
The charity provides winter warmth packages and seasonal food aid across multiple countries.
The organization builds modular homes and maintains a high TB cure rate.
Mercy-USA supports visually impaired children in Gaza and provides food aid in Syria.
Donors can contribute through various methods specifically designated for Zakat.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Mercy-USA earns a strong overall rating due to its high program efficiency and direct service impact, though its score is tempered by low working capital reserves. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, aligning well with donors seeking humanitarian urgency and religious compliance.
areas_for_improvement: Limited liquidity with only 0.8 months of working capital available to cover operating expenses.
Enhancing long-term financial reserves to improve organizational resilience during funding fluctuations.

dimension_explanations.alignment: Mercy-USA shows high alignment for Muslim donors as a zakat-eligible entity that adheres to specific asnaf categories for distribution [2][9]. Its focus on urgent humanitarian crises, such as the Syria humanitarian crisis and food insecurity in Somalia, establishes a strong track record of addressing high-urgency needs [4][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high impact through direct service delivery, such as maintaining high TB cure rates in Somalia and providing safe water infrastructure to thousands of beneficiaries [6][8]. Its financial health is underscored by a Charity Navigator score of 92.33333333333333/100, reflecting strong accountability and a 96.3% program expense ratio [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development provides life-saving nutrition, health services, and sustainable water infrastructure to vulnerable populations across Somalia, Syria, and beyond.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 96.3%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach beneficiaries.
Highly efficient fundraising operations, spending only $0.03 to raise every $1.
Strong track record in specialized health services, including high TB cure rates and modular housing projects.
baseline_narrative.summary: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development, Inc. is a humanitarian organization dedicated to crisis relief and sustainable development, specifically focusing on nutrition support for children and mothers in Somalia [3][4]. The organization maintains a high level of financial efficiency, reporting a total revenue of $34,922,507 while ensuring that 96.3% of its expenses are directed toward its core programs [1][3].

The charity experienced a <cite id="18">3-year revenue CAGR of -8.6%</cite>, indicating a recent contraction in funding that may impact future scale.

case_against.summary: The primary concern is financial resilience; the organization has <cite id="17">very low working capital</cite> and has seen a <cite id="18">revenue decline of -8.6%</cite> over three years.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023; ratings from late 2024
confidence.notes: Strong alignment between third-party ratings and internal reports.

alignment.explanation: There is strong alignment for Muslim donors through <cite id="8">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and a focus on <cite id="10">crisis zones like Gaza and Yemen</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be further enhanced by <cite id="23">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Mercy-USA is a highly credible institution with <cite id="1">over 35 years of experience</cite> and <cite id="19">100% independent board members</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization could <cite id="20">publish more consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for better cost-effectiveness analysis.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="21">96.3% program expense ratio</cite> (per Form 990) and a <cite id="22">documented theory of change</cite> focused on health and water.
impact.improvement: The charity should <cite id="17">increase operating reserves</cite> from the current 0.8 months to at least 3 months to ensure long-term stability.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Verified outcomes and documented theory of change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Distributes majority of grants to foreign recipients for international relief.

rich_narrative.headline: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development provides life-saving nutrition, health services, and sustainable water infrastructure to vulnerable populations across Somalia, Syria, and beyond.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support a long-standing Muslim-led institution with exceptional fundraising efficiency and a focus on health and water infrastructure in crisis zones.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through an organization with a 35-year track record
Value low overhead and high fundraising efficiency ($0.02 per $1)
Prioritize direct medical and water interventions over advocacy
giving_considerations: The organization is currently in a contraction phase with declining revenue
Low cash reserves mean the charity is highly dependent on immediate donations
Most impact is delivered through direct service clinics and infrastructure
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking organizations with large financial endowments or those looking for domestic US-based advocacy.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The charity tracks specific outcome metrics like TB cure rates and water beneficiaries, though it lacks independent RCTs.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Mercy-USA focuses on improving health, nutrition, and water access to promote long-term economic and educational growth.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking metrics like TB cure rates ensures that medical interventions are actually saving lives, not just providing supplies.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With declining revenue and low reserves, the organization has significant capacity to absorb new funding to stabilize operations.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Somalia, Gaza, Yemen, Kenya, Albania, Pakistan

peer_comparison.differentiator: Mercy-USA stands out for its <cite id="11">$0.02 fundraising efficiency</cite>, which is significantly better than the <cite id="25">90.3% peer program ratio median</cite> context.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar focus on healthcare direct service
similar_organizations.name: Heal Palestine

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates elite fiscal discipline, spending only <cite id="11">$0.03 per $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly better than industry averages.
According to Charity Navigator, the organization directs <cite id="12">96.3% of its expenses toward programs</cite>, ensuring donor funds reach those in need.
Mercy-USA maintains a high <cite id="5">80% TB cure rate</cite> and provides critical nutrition for <cite id="13">children and nursing mothers in Somalia</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: Mercy-USA's operational model prioritizes direct service delivery in high-need regions. Their infrastructure projects are substantial, including <cite id="14">water berkads and infrastructure serving 10,000 people</cite> in 2023 alone. This is complemented by seasonal relief efforts that provided <cite id="15">food aid across 10 countries</cite> and winter warmth packages to vulnerable households. Their commitment to transparency is verified by a <cite id="16">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level available.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1988</cite>, Mercy-USA for Aid and Development is a veteran humanitarian organization that specializes in health, nutrition, and sustainable water infrastructure. The organization operates with a high degree of financial efficiency, maintaining an <cite id="2">overall score of 92.92.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>. In 2023, the charity reported <cite id="3">$54,067,823 in total revenue</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="4">$53,694,916 in public contributions</cite>. Mercy-USA is particularly noted for its specialized medical interventions, such as achieving an <cite id="5">80% TB cure rate</cite> through its clinics and constructing <cite id="6">950 modular homes</cite> for displaced families. Their water programs are equally robust, having <cite id="7">dug or repaired 800 wells</cite> to provide long-term access to safe water. The organization is <cite id="8">zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the <cite id="9">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> asnaf categories. While their reach is global, including significant operations in <cite id="10">Gaza, Somalia, and Yemen</cite>, they maintain a lean administrative profile, spending only <cite id="11">$0.02 to raise every $1</cite>.
